skill.name: extract-transcripts
skill.description: Extract readable markdown transcripts from Claude Code and Codex CLI JSONL session files, with optional DuckDB indexing for search.
skill.summary: Convert Claude Code and Codex session logs into readable markdown transcripts.
skill.author: 0xBigBoss

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are mostly documentation examples, markdown output formatting, SQL text, or line-reference text rather than executable dangerous code. The confirmed risk is privacy exposure from reading hidden Claude and Codex transcript stores and writing extracted conversation content or a DuckDB index to local files.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Local Transcript Indexing
medium_findings.description: The indexer searches default hidden Claude session directories, recursively reads JSONL transcript files, and stores message content in a local DuckDB database. This is legitimate for transcript search, but the database can contain private prompts, assistant responses, file paths, project names, and tool metadata.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented default paths and code confirm hidden session directories are read and indexed into a persistent database. No network exfiltration was found, so the risk is privacy exposure through local storage rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Transcript Export Can Persist Sensitive Conversation Data
medium_findings.description: The Claude transcript extractor writes user-selected markdown output and can include tool inputs and thinking blocks. This is expected behavior, but exported transcripts may contain secrets, private code, command output, or internal project context copied from session logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly formats optional thinking and tool data, then writes the transcript to a user-selected file. This is a clear local data handling risk, although it requires intentional CLI use and does not show exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Command Execution Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported command-execution locations are markdown command examples or generated markdown code blocks, not shell execution by the Python scripts. The reviewed scripts do not call subprocess, os.system, eval, or exec for command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation examples or string formatting for output. Targeted search found no command execution APIs in the scripts.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography lines do not implement cryptography. They are headings, parser setup, SQL ordering text, or commit hash display logic used for transcript metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found no hashing or encryption operation at the cited locations. One Codex metadata path displays a shortened commit hash, which is not used for security.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are Benign Metadata Output
low_findings.description: The scripts read and print metadata such as working directory, git branch, model, file path, and CLI version. This is useful transcript context, but users should avoid sharing generated output publicly without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The metadata extraction is explicit and local, and it supports the advertised transcript functionality. The impact is limited to disclosure if exported transcripts or indexes are shared.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Session Directory Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents and implements reads from hidden Claude and Codex session locations under the user home directory. This should be disclosed because those files can contain sensitive conversation data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Both the README-style instructions and indexer defaults point to hidden session locations. The behavior is intentional and local.

dangerous_patterns.title: Persistent Local Database of Message Content
dangerous_patterns.description: The indexer stores parsed message content, file paths, timestamps, model names, and tool names in a DuckDB file. This increases the lifetime and searchability of sensitive transcript data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The insert statements and database setup clearly persist message and tool data locally. There is no evidence of remote transfer.

content.value_statement: Claude Code and Codex sessions are stored as JSONL files that are hard to read directly. This skill converts them into readable markdown and can index sessions for local search.
actual_capabilities: Extracts readable markdown from Claude Code session JSONL files.
Extracts readable markdown from Codex CLI rollout and history files.
Optionally includes Claude tool calls and thinking blocks in output.
Processes one file or all JSONL files in a directory.
Creates a DuckDB index for recent-session listing and text search.
Filters indexed sessions by project path, date range, and search text.
limitations: It reads local transcript files only and does not recover deleted sessions.
DuckDB must be installed separately before using the indexer.
Codex extraction truncates very long messages in the readable output.
Exported transcripts may contain private prompts, code, paths, and tool data.

use_cases.title: Review a Development Session
use_cases.description: Convert one Claude Code session into markdown for review, handoff, or documentation.

use_cases.title: Search Past Assistant Work
use_cases.description: Index many sessions and search for previous decisions, errors, commands, or implementation notes.

use_cases.title: Audit AI Tool Usage
use_cases.description: Summarize session metadata and tool usage across local transcript files.

prompt_templates.title: Extract One Session
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a single known Claude Code session file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the extract-transcripts skill to convert this Claude Code JSONL session into a readable markdown transcript: <session file path>.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Short Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick review without reading the full transcript.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the extract-transcripts skill to summarize this session only. Include date, duration, message counts, and the first substantive prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Include Tool Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review where tool calls matter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the extract-transcripts skill to extract this Claude Code session with tool calls included. Save the result as a markdown file.

prompt_templates.title: Index and Search Sessions
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow across many local sessions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the extract-transcripts indexer to index my local sessions, then search for sessions that mention <topic> within the last 7 days.

output_examples.input: A Claude Code JSONL session file from a project directory.
output_examples.output: A markdown transcript with session date, duration, model, working directory, user messages, assistant messages, and optional tool call summaries.
A readable list of recent Codex prompts grouped by session and timestamp, with long entries shortened for review.

output_examples.input: A local transcript index search for an error message.
output_examples.output: A list of matching sessions with dates, project names, file paths, and short message snippets around each match.

best_practices: Review transcripts for secrets before sharing them outside your machine.
Use summary mode first when you only need a quick session overview.
Store the DuckDB index in a protected local directory with appropriate permissions.
anti_patterns: Do not publish extracted transcripts without removing private prompts, code, and paths.
Do not include thinking blocks unless you have a clear review need.
Do not index directories that contain unrelated or untrusted JSONL files.

faq.question: What session formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Claude Code JSONL sessions and Codex CLI rollout or history JSONL files.

faq.question: Does it send transcripts to a remote service?
faq.answer: No evidence of network transfer was found. The scripts read and write local files only.

faq.question: Can it include tool calls?
faq.answer: Yes. Claude Code extraction can include tool call names and inputs when requested.

faq.question: Where is the transcript index stored?
faq.answer: By default, the indexer stores a DuckDB database under the local Claude transcript index directory.

faq.question: Is DuckDB required for every feature?
faq.answer: No. DuckDB is required for indexing and search, but direct transcript extraction uses standard Python libraries.

faq.question: What should I check before sharing output?
faq.answer: Check for secrets, private code, working directories, branch names, prompts, and tool input details.
